items.final_wording: Supports noting pregnancy, obesity, diabetes, reflux, obstruction, emergency, and difficult-airway context as assessment qualifiers only.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for Preoperative Fasting and the Use of Pharmacologic Agents to Reduce the Risk of Pulmonary Aspiration
source.population: Healthy patients of all ages undergoing elective procedures requiring anesthesia or sedation, with stated modifying conditions.
source.setting: Preprocedure fasting assessment for elective anesthesia or sedation.
source.exclusions: Exact for preoperative record review, interview, fasting compliance, and aspiration-risk modifiers. Fasting durations, medicine instructions, and management are not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports noting pregnancy, obesity, diabetes, reflux, obstruction, emergency, and difficult-airway context as assessment qualifiers only.

items.rationale: The ASA section explicitly identifies obesity as a modifier requiring clinician assessment in fasting and aspiration-risk context; it does not create fasting or medicine instructions.
